perturbation.original_text: Each party shall take reasonable measures to protect the secrecy of and avoid disclosure and unauthorized use of the Confidential Information of the other party. Without limiting the foregoing, each party shall take at least those measures that it takes to protect its own most highly confidential information, and shall promptly notify the disclosing party of any misuse or misappropriation of Confidential Information of which it becomes aware.
perturbation.changed_text: Each party shall take reasonable measures to protect the secrecy of and avoid disclosure and unauthorized use of the Confidential Information of the other party. Without limiting the foregoing, each party shall take at least those measures that it takes to protect its own confidential information, but notification to the disclosing party of any misuse or misappropriation of Confidential Information is optional.
perturbation.explanation: The change introduces an in-text contradiction by making notification of misuse optional, while the original text mandates prompt notification. This contradicts the overall intent of maintaining confidentiality, particularly the section on remedies, where violation of the agreement can lead to legal action. Now, because notification is optional it is contradicting the secrecy of the information
perturbation.contradicted_text: Maintenance of Confidentiality . Each party shall take reasonable measures to protect the secrecy of and avoid disclosure and unauthorized use of the Confidential Information of the other party. Without limiting the foregoing, each party shall take at least those measures that it takes to protect its own confidential information, but notification to the disclosing party of any misuse or misappropriation of Confidential Information is optional. Each party sh all disclose Confidential Information only to those officers, directors, employees and contractors who are required to have the information in order to evaluate or engage in discussions concerning the contemplated business relationship, and such party shal l remain responsible for compliance with the terms of this Agreement by its officers, directors, employees and contractors.
perturbation.justification: The changed text makes notification of misuse of confidential information optional, which directly contradicts the original intent of the agreement to protect confidentiality and the obligation to notify the disclosing party. The contradicted text further reinforces the importance of maintaining confidentiality. By making notification optional, the change creates a clear inconsistency regarding the responsibility of each party to protect and report misuse of confidential information, which would lead to confusion and potentially make the contract unenforceable.

perturbation.original_text: Parties agree to honor all patent protections.
perturbation.changed_text: Parties are encouraged, but not obligated, to honor all patent protections.
perturbation.explanation: The change from "agree to honor" to "are encouraged, but not obligated, to honor" introduces an in-text contradiction. The original text creates a firm commitment to respect patent protections, whereas the change makes honoring patents optional. This impacts the strength of the IP protections within the agreement, directly contradicts the 'No License' section
perturbation.contradicted_text: Term . The obligations of each receiving party hereunder shall surviv e for a period of one year after the disclosure of the Confidential Information, notwithstanding if the Confidential Information of the other party disclosed hereunder becomes publicly known and made generally available through no action or inaction of the receiving party. Parties are encouraged, but not obligated, to honor all patent protections.
perturbation.justification: The original text stated that parties "agree to honor all patent protections", creating a firm commitment. The changed text modifies this to "are encouraged, but not obligated, to honor all patent protections", making patent protection optional. This directly contradicts any clause expecting mandatory patent protections, as the changed text effectively nullifies any requirement to respect patents. The contradicted text contains the changed text which now makes the obligation to honor all patent protections optional, fundamentally weakening the agreement and creating a clear contradiction.
